channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: MacKenzie Scott donates record $20 million to Active Minds to expand youth mental health support across the US
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Trump fires final two US election commissioners ahead of 2026 midterms polls
item.description: The move sparked criticism from Democratic leaders and election officials, who argued that removing the agency's remaining commissioners so close to the 2026 midterm elections could disrupt election administration. Arizona secretary of state Adrian Fontes described the decision as "irresponsible and dangerous." "This administration remains dead set on causing chaos for our election officials across the country," he said in a press release.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Bonnie Tyler, who topped the charts with epic 'Total Eclipse of the Heart', dies
Florida airport officially renamed after Donald Trump; US Prez calls it ‘a very big day’
item.description: Palm Beach International Airport officially became President Donald J. Trump International Airport on Thursday. The renaming legislation was signed earlier this year by Florida Governor Ron DeSantis. Trump Force One, owned by The Trump Organisation, was the first aircraft to land. Eric Trump expressed pride in the honor bestowed upon his father. The renaming is part of a broader effort to place Trump's name on public infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Pak's remittances of $41. 6bn exceed its total exports
Won't tolerate any misuse of H-1B visa, says Vance as US launches fraud probe
Ukrainian drones batter Russian oil facilities, set more oil tankers ablaze
Iran's Khamenei buried in Mashhad four months after US-Israeli strikes killed him
item.description: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was buried in Mashhad after a week of ceremonies. He ruled Iran for nearly thirty-seven years and died in recent airstrikes. Large crowds gathered for funeral processions which brought daily life to a standstill. His eldest son led prayers, but his successor was notably absent from the burial. This burial occurred amid renewed hostilities and recent strikes between the US and Iran.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Candace Owens under fire from conservatives; Tyler Robinson-Charlie Kirk case comments trigger fresh controversy
item.description: Candace Owens is facing criticism from several conservative commentators after questioning the case against Tyler Robinson, who is accused of killing Charlie Kirk. Critics including Ben Shapiro, Laura Loomer and Blake Neff say Owens has unfairly challenged evidence presented in court. The dispute intensified as prosecutors introduced testimony, surveillance footage and other evidence during Robinson’s preliminary hearing.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Andrew Tate links Connor Murphy’s death to Ayahuasca, claims psychedelic left influencer ‘insane’ before tragedy
item.description: Andrew Tate has linked Connor Murphy’s death to the influencer’s past use of Ayahuasca, claiming the psychedelic substance affected his mental health. His comments sparked debate online, with some users agreeing and others criticizing his remarks. However, authorities have not confirmed any connection between Murphy’s death and drug use. Autopsy and toxicology results are still awaited.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Quote of the day by Franz Kafka: 'There are some things one can achieve only by a deliberate leap in the opposite direction'
item.description: True progress sometimes emerges from taking steps that seem counterproductive at first glance. Kafka's philosophy hints that, occasionally, achieving certain aspirations requires not only perseverance but also a deliberate detour. This notion extends into areas like psychology and artistic pursuits. In our rapidly evolving modern world, the strategy of intentional breaks becomes increasingly significant. Unlike aimlessly abandoning a direction, a well-considered pivot can yield transformative results.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Blind professor catches massive AI cheating scandal at Brown University, says it should be a wake-up call after scores drop from 100 to 48
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Diogenes: 'I threw away my cup when I saw a child drinking water from his hands'
item.description: Diogenes, the radical philosopher, famously threw away his cup when he witnessed a child quenching thirst using only their hands. This timeless tale encourages us to challenge our reliance on luxuries and conveniences. Cynic philosophy suggests that true freedom is found in having fewer possessions. In today's complex world, embracing simplicity can enhance our well-being, reminding us that valuable insights often arise from keen observation rather than formal learning.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Charlie Kirk: 'I can't stand the word empathy. I think it is a made-up, new-age term'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Billionaire Melinda Gates could have filled an entire room in her 66,000-square-foot mansion with Hermès Birkin bags. But when her daughter Jennifer wanted a luxury purse, she chose to say no
item.description: Melinda French Gates has ingrained the principle of mindful spending into her children's upbringing, emphasizing that wealth should not lead to impulsive indulgence. Through an innovative allowance system, she nurtured habits of saving and early philanthropy. Additionally, the family prioritized hands-on experiences to expand their understanding of the world, ultimately aiming to cultivate a sense of self-worth that transcends their substantial inheritance.
item.dc:creator: TOI Tech Desk

item.title: What is coincidence? Quote of the day by Carl Jung: 'We often dream about people from whom we receive a letter by the next post'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists use AI to decode sperm whale communication, discovering a possible phonetic alphabet and revealing that Mediterranean sperm whales have different dialects by region
item.description: In a groundbreaking study, scientists harnessed the power of artificial intelligence to decode the intricate communication system of sperm whales. Through their research, they discovered a phonetic alphabet and combinations of clicks that resemble words, revealing a unique regional dialect among sperm whales in the eastern Mediterranean Sea. This revelation transforms our understanding of non-human communication and highlights the importance of these vocalizations for conservation efforts.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Marcus Aurelius: "The happiness of your life depends upon..." - a timeless reminder that a peaceful mind shapes a happier life
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Queen Elizabeth: “Football’s a difficult business, and aren’t they prima donnas? But it’s a wonderful game.” - quote that perfectly captures why football remains the world's favourite sport
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Charlie Kirk killing: Tyler Robinson told partner Lance Twiggs he regreted the shooting, video played in court
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
